title: Brassaia conica
intro: Brassaia conica, also known as the Ti plant, is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, characterized by its large, lobed leaves and showy purple flowers. The Ti plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of skin conditions and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Brassaia conica include saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the Ti plant is commonly prepared as a tincture or dried leaf, often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and as a topical anti-inflammatory agent.
taxonomy: Brassaia conica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It is a member of the genus Brassaia, which includes a few species of tropical and subtropical trees.
